<!--
PR title — Conventional-Commit style: type(scope): description
Scopes: desktop · protocol · design · release · docs · tests

Show your work with PwrSnap. The clearest PRs include a picture (and, for
anything with motion, a recording). PwrSnap makes both in seconds — and if
you don't have it yet, that's the best reason to install it: https://pwrsnap.com
-->

## Summary
<!-- What does this change, and why? -->

## Screenshots
<!--
Show the change. Fastest path: capture with PwrSnap, click the **Med** copy
button, and paste (⌘V) here. For UI changes, before/after pairs are gold —
PwrSnap's annotation tools (arrows, highlights, callouts) make the diff obvious.
-->

## Screen recording
<!--
For anything with motion or a multi-step flow, a recording beats stills.
Record it with PwrSnap and paste it in:
- **GIF** — short interactions (a few seconds): drops inline and autoplays.
- **MP4** — longer workflows: GitHub embeds a player.
-->

## How was this tested?
<!-- Commands you ran (pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, e2e), plus any manual steps. -->

## Checklist
- [ ] PR title follows `type(scope): description`
- [ ] Tests added or updated (or explained why this is docs-only)
- [ ] Relevant checks pass locally (`pnpm test`, `pnpm typecheck`)
- [ ] `THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES` regenerated if dependencies changed (`pnpm licenses:generate`)
- [ ] Screenshot or recording attached for user-facing changes
